note: golden_hash filled by golden-parity.test.mjs --update. Vector 1: EU vertical-slice retention exactly at the 5% threshold, compliant. Vector 2: EU horizontal first-loss retention below 5%, noncompliant. Vector 3: US Reg RR retention meets 5% numerically but the retainer fails the EU sole-purpose test AND the retained interest was hedged -- both structural breaches fire even though the percentage passes (only meaningful under EU jurisdiction; included to prove the structural gates are independent of the percentage gate). Vector 4: US QRM-exempt pool, zero securitized exposure and zero retained amount -- finite gate (null ratio, not Infinity/NaN) and the QRM exemption zeroing the required percentage so a zero-retention pool is still compliant.

vectors.name: eu_vertical_slice_at_threshold_compliant
vectors.description: EU jurisdiction, vertical-slice retention. Total securitized exposure EUR2000M, retained EUR100M = exactly 5.00%, no sole-purpose or hedging flags. Compliant.

output_payload.regulatory_basis: EU Securitisation Regulation (EU) 2017/2402 Art.6 (originator/sponsor/original-lender net economic interest, ongoing 5% retention, no hedging/sale, sole-purpose-entity prohibition); U.S. Credit Risk Retention Rule (Dodd-Frank Sec.941, Reg RR, 12 CFR Part 244) 5% base retention with Sec_.19 Qualified Residential Mortgage exemption.
output_payload.note: Deterministic structural check from caller-supplied retention method, exposure/retained nominal amounts, and jurisdiction-specific flags for a single reporting date.

vectors.name: eu_horizontal_below_threshold_noncompliant
vectors.description: EU jurisdiction, horizontal first-loss retention. Total securitized exposure EUR1500M, retained EUR45M = 3.00%, below the 5% requirement. Noncompliant.

output_payload.regulatory_basis: EU Securitisation Regulation (EU) 2017/2402 Art.6 (originator/sponsor/original-lender net economic interest, ongoing 5% retention, no hedging/sale, sole-purpose-entity prohibition); U.S. Credit Risk Retention Rule (Dodd-Frank Sec.941, Reg RR, 12 CFR Part 244) 5% base retention with Sec_.19 Qualified Residential Mortgage exemption.
output_payload.note: Deterministic structural check from caller-supplied retention method, exposure/retained nominal amounts, and jurisdiction-specific flags for a single reporting date.

vectors.name: eu_sole_purpose_and_hedging_breach
vectors.description: EU jurisdiction, L-shaped retention meeting the 5% numeric threshold (EUR1000M / EUR50M = 5%), but the retainer is flagged as a sole-purpose securitisation entity AND the retained interest was hedged -- both structural gates fire independently of the percentage pass.

output_payload.regulatory_basis: EU Securitisation Regulation (EU) 2017/2402 Art.6 (originator/sponsor/original-lender net economic interest, ongoing 5% retention, no hedging/sale, sole-purpose-entity prohibition); U.S. Credit Risk Retention Rule (Dodd-Frank Sec.941, Reg RR, 12 CFR Part 244) 5% base retention with Sec_.19 Qualified Residential Mortgage exemption.
output_payload.note: Deterministic structural check from caller-supplied retention method, exposure/retained nominal amounts, and jurisdiction-specific flags for a single reporting date.

vectors.name: us_qrm_exempt_zero_input_edge
vectors.description: US jurisdiction, all exposures QRM-qualified (Reg RR Sec_.19 exemption), zero securitized exposure and zero retained amount. Finite gate: ratio resolves to null (not Infinity/NaN); required retention percentage zeroes out under the QRM exemption so a zero-retention pool is still compliant.

output_payload.regulatory_basis: EU Securitisation Regulation (EU) 2017/2402 Art.6 (originator/sponsor/original-lender net economic interest, ongoing 5% retention, no hedging/sale, sole-purpose-entity prohibition); U.S. Credit Risk Retention Rule (Dodd-Frank Sec.941, Reg RR, 12 CFR Part 244) 5% base retention with Sec_.19 Qualified Residential Mortgage exemption.
output_payload.note: Deterministic structural check from caller-supplied retention method, exposure/retained nominal amounts, and jurisdiction-specific flags for a single reporting date.
